title: URL Decoder
shortDescription: Decode percent-encoded URLs and query strings into readable text.
metaDescription: Decode percent-encoded URLs, query strings, and path segments into plain readable text in your browser.
intro: Paste an encoded URL or query string to see the decoded text immediately.
overview:
Use this tool to turn percent-encoded text such as %20, %3F, and %26 back into readable characters. It is helpful when inspecting links, request parameters, redirect targets, or copied strings that include encoded punctuation.

The decoder treats the input as text and converts valid escape sequences into their matching characters. If the text contains malformed sequences, those parts may remain unchanged or produce an error depending on the characters entered. That makes it useful for checking exactly how a URL fragment will be interpreted before you share, store, or debug it.
howItWorks: Paste the encoded URL, path, or query string into the input.
The tool decodes percent-escapes and returns readable text immediately.
Review the result and compare it with the original string if you need to spot encoding issues.

useCases.title: Inspect a copied link
useCases.description: Decode a link copied from chat, email, or logs to read the real path and parameters without the percent signs.

useCases.title: Check query parameters
useCases.description: Reveal parameter names and values such as redirect targets, search terms, or filters hidden inside an encoded query string.

useCases.title: Review redirect destinations
useCases.description: Decode callback or redirect URLs before using them in code, tests, or security reviews.

examples.title: Decode a query string
examples.description: Shows how encoded punctuation and spaces become normal readable text.

examples.title: Decode a path with reserved characters
examples.description: Useful when a URL path and its parameters are stored as one encoded string.

examples.title: Decode a return URL
examples.description: Shows that double-encoded pieces remain partially encoded after a single decode.

faq.question: Does this tool decode plus signs as spaces?
faq.answer: Only percent-escapes are decoded. A literal plus sign stays as + unless the input already represents a space that way in its source context.

faq.question: Why does part of my string stay encoded after decoding?
faq.answer: Some inputs are encoded more than once. A single pass turns %2520 into %20, but a second pass would be needed to get an actual space.

faq.question: Can I decode a full URL, not just a query string?
faq.answer: Yes. You can paste a complete URL, but the tool will only change valid encoded sequences and leave the rest of the URL structure intact.

faq.question: What happens with invalid percent sequences?
faq.answer: Characters that are not valid escapes may be left unchanged or may cause the decoder to reject the input, depending on the sequence and browser behavior.

seo.title: URL Decoder Tool
seo.description: Decode percent-encoded URLs, query strings, and path segments into plain readable text in your browser.
